thread.title: AAP Celebrates Victories in Punjab Bypolls While Facing Challenges in Barnala | Chandigarh News - Times of India
thread.title_full: AAP Celebrates Victories in Punjab Bypolls While Facing Challenges in Barnala | Chandigarh News - Times of India

author: TNN
title: AAP Celebrates Victories in Punjab Bypolls While Facing Challenges in Barnala | Chandigarh News - Times of India
text: Chandigarh: Even as the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) rejoiced the fall of Congress bastions Dera Baba Nanak and Gidderbaha, it would feel the need to introspect about what went wrong in its own stronghold, Barnala. Results of the assembly byelections come a day after Sunam MLA and Punjab minister Aman Arora was named the state unit president of AAP. His next challenge would be the upcoming civic body elections in five key cities of Jalandhar, Ludhiana, Patiala, Amritsar and Phagwara.115612019 “From being represented by Meet Hayer, who was a minister, to now being represented by an MLA of the opposition Congress, the dynamics have changed for Barnala. Party leadership will certainly reflect on why voters chose Congress. There could be a gap in the way the AAP govt’s initiatives taken in the last 2.5 years were showcased during the campaign,” said an AAP leader, requesting anonymity.Victory in three of the four seats which went to bypolls was still a celebration for AAP. CM Bhagwant Mann said, “Many congratulations to Punjabis for the spectacular win in the byelections! Under the leadership of Arvind Kejriwal, AAP is reaching new heights, working day and night with honesty and without discrimination for the progress and prosperity of Punjab. We will prioritise fulfilling every promise made to the people during the byelections.”Punjab minister Harjot Singh Bains targeted Congress leadership, saying: “In Gidderbaha and Dera Baba Nanak, repeated victories had inflated the egos of Raja Warring and Sukhjinder Randhawa, but today, the people defeated their arrogance. It is a victory for our welfare schemes.” As the results started coming in, party office in Chandigarh came alive with drumbeats and distribution of sweets.Was 2022’s unfinished business, says MannMann and Punjab AAP president Aman Arora reached Delhi to join Arvind Kejriwal. Mann highlighted that AAP had won three Congress seats, “which AAP had not won even in the big wave of 2022”. “The BJP lost security deposit on three seats and barely managed to save it on the fourth (Giddarbaha),” he said.We also published the following articles recentlyMinister Aman Arora replaces CM Bhagwant Mann as president of AAPs Punjab unitThe Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in Punjab has appointed cabinet minister Aman Arora as the new state unit president, replacing Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann. This strategic move aims to strengthen the party's appeal among Hindu voters, a demographic where the BJP has made inroads.115586790Punjab bypoll results: AAP wins 3 seats, loses from its stronghold to CongressPunjab's ruling AAP won three out of four assembly by-elections, strengthening its hold on the state. The victories in Chabbewal, Dera Baba Nanak, and Gidderbaha are seen as a public endorsement of AAP's governance. However, Congress managed to secure a win in Barnala. The by-elections were triggered by four sitting MLAs winning parliamentary seats earlier this year.115594362Punjab bypolls: Clash breaks out between Congress, AAP in Dera Pathana villageA clash erupted between Congress and AAP workers at a Gurdaspur polling booth during the Dera Baba Nanak by-elections. Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa urged voters to maintain peace, alleging an attempted booth capture by individuals he deemed as \"goons.\" By-elections are underway in four Punjab assembly constituencies, including Dera Baba Nanak, with results expected on November 23rd.115476368
